lineTexts.text: The inferior man works through power. The superior man does not act thus. To continue is dangerous. A goat butts against a hedge and gets its horns entangled.
lineTexts.interpretation: Using power aggressively leads to entanglement. The wise avoid such crude demonstrations of force.

lineTexts.text: Perseverance brings good fortune. Remorse disappears. The hedge opens; there is no entanglement. Power depends upon the axle of a big cart.
lineTexts.interpretation: Obstacles dissolve for one who perseveres with true power—power that is grounded and moving in the right direction.

lineTexts.text: Loses the goat with ease. No remorse.
lineTexts.interpretation: The stubborn, pushing energy dissolves. Letting go of aggressive tendencies comes easily and brings no regret.

lineTexts.text: A goat butts against a hedge. It cannot go backward, it cannot go forward. Nothing serves to further. If one notes the difficulty, this brings good fortune.
lineTexts.interpretation: Power has pushed forward too far and is stuck. Recognizing the difficulty allows for change, which ultimately brings good fortune.

judgment:
PROGRESS. The powerful prince
Is honored with horses in large numbers.
In a single day he is granted audience three times.
image:
The sun rises over the earth:
The image of PROGRESS.
Thus the superior man himself
Brightens his bright virtue.
commentary:
The hexagram represents the sun rising over the earth. It is therefore the symbol of rapid, easy progress, which at the same time means ever-widening expansion and clarity.

The ruler is pictured as a powerful prince whose authority raises him above the nobility and leads to exceptional recognition. A person in an expanding position is granted special access and receives extraordinary gifts as rewards for their virtue.

lineTexts.text: Progressing, but turned back. Perseverance brings good fortune. If one meets with no confidence, one should remain calm. No mistake.
lineTexts.interpretation: Initial attempts at progress are rebuffed. Remain calm and steadfast despite lack of recognition. This brings no blame.

lineTexts.text: Progressing, but in sorrow. Perseverance brings good fortune. Then one obtains great happiness from one's ancestress.
lineTexts.interpretation: Progress comes despite obstacles. Steadfast effort brings blessing from a source of maternal wisdom and tradition.

lineTexts.text: All are in accord. Remorse disappears.
lineTexts.interpretation: Progress gains support from all directions. Previous regrets dissolve as the way forward becomes clear and supported.

lineTexts.text: Progress like a hamster. Perseverance brings danger.
lineTexts.interpretation: Progressing by hoarding and grasping, like a hamster. Such behavior, even if persistent, brings danger. Change approach.

lineTexts.text: Remorse disappears. Take not gain and loss to heart. Undertakings bring good fortune. Everything serves to further.
lineTexts.interpretation: Regret vanishes when one stops calculating gains and losses. Acting freely from pure motivation, everything furthers progress.

lineTexts.text: Making progress with the horns is permissible only for the purpose of punishing one's own city. To be conscious of danger brings good fortune. No blame. Perseverance brings humiliation.
lineTexts.interpretation: Aggressive progress is appropriate only for correcting one's own domain. Awareness of the dangers involved brings good fortune; stubborn persistence brings shame.

judgment:
DARKENING OF THE LIGHT. In adversity
It furthers one to be persevering.
image:
The light has sunk into the earth:
The image of DARKENING OF THE LIGHT.
Thus does the superior man live with the great mass:
He veils his light, yet still shines.
commentary:
Here the sun has sunk under the earth and is therefore darkened. The name of the hexagram means literally "wounding of the bright"; hence the individual lines contain frequent references to wounding.

The situation is one in which a person of genuine ability must hide their capabilities because of dark conditions. They must conceal their light, appearing simple and ordinary, while inwardly maintaining clarity. One must not let adversity undermine inner worth.

lineTexts.text: Darkening of the light during flight. He lowers his wings. The superior man does not eat for three days on his wanderings. But he has somewhere to go. The host has occasion to gossip about him.
lineTexts.interpretation: In retreat, the wise person endures hardship. Though criticized by those who don't understand, one perseveres toward a clear goal.

lineTexts.text: Darkening of the light injures him in the left thigh. He gives aid with the strength of a horse. Good fortune.
lineTexts.interpretation: Wounded but still able to help others with vigor. Despite personal injury, lending strength to rescue those in need brings good fortune.

lineTexts.text: Darkening of the light during the hunt in the south. Their great leader is captured. One must not expect perseverance too soon.
lineTexts.interpretation: In pursuing the source of darkness, the main offender is found. But complete reform cannot be expected immediately; patience is needed.

lineTexts.text: He penetrates the left side of the belly. One gets at the very heart of the darkening of the light, and leaves gate and courtyard.
lineTexts.interpretation: One perceives the inner nature of the darkness and, understanding it fully, chooses to withdraw completely from the corrupt environment.

lineTexts.text: Darkening of the light as with Prince Chi. Perseverance furthers.
lineTexts.interpretation: Like Prince Chi who feigned madness to survive under a tyrant, concealing one's true nature may be necessary. Inner constancy must be maintained.

lineTexts.text: Not light but darkness. First he climbed up to heaven, then he plunged into the depths of the earth.
lineTexts.interpretation: The source of darkness has reached its peak and now falls. What rose to the heights through evil means inevitably descends to destruction.

judgment: THE FAMILY. The perseverance of the woman furthers.
image:
Wind comes forth from fire:
The image of THE FAMILY.
Thus the superior man has substance in his words
And duration in his way of life.
commentary:
The foundation of the family is the relationship between husband and wife. The tie that holds the family together lies in the loyalty and perseverance of the wife. The place of the family in society is represented by the trigrams: fire within, wind outside.

The family is society in embryo; it is the native soil on which performance of moral duty is made easy through natural affection. The laws of the family apply universally. Each person must cultivate the virtues that make a household thrive.

lineTexts.text: Firm seclusion within the family. Remorse disappears.
lineTexts.interpretation: Clear boundaries and proper order within the family from the start prevent later regrets. Early discipline creates lasting harmony.

lineTexts.text: She should not follow her whims. She must attend within to the food. Perseverance brings good fortune.
lineTexts.interpretation: Attending to the essential nourishment—both physical and spiritual—of the family brings good fortune. Focus on duties, not personal desires.

lineTexts.text: When tempers flare up in the family, too great severity brings remorse. Good fortune nonetheless. When woman and child dally and laugh, it leads in the end to humiliation.
lineTexts.interpretation: Strictness may cause regret, but it's better than excessive leniency. Discipline that seems harsh is preferable to disorder.

lineTexts.text: She is the treasure of the house. Great good fortune.
lineTexts.interpretation: One who maintains the household wisely, creating prosperity through care and management, is the true treasure of the family.

lineTexts.text: As a king he approaches his family. Fear not. Good fortune.
lineTexts.interpretation: Leadership in the family with love rather than fear. When the head of the household leads with virtue and affection, all goes well.

lineTexts.text: His work commands respect. In the end good fortune comes.
lineTexts.interpretation: When one's character and work command genuine respect, the family flourishes. Inner truth brings outward recognition.

judgment: OPPOSITION. In small matters, good fortune.
image:
Above, fire; below, the lake:
The image of OPPOSITION.
Thus amid all fellowship
The superior man retains his individuality.
commentary:
This hexagram is composed of the trigram Li above, meaning fire, and the trigram Dui below, meaning the lake. Fire moves upward and water moves downward—they move in opposite directions.

Opposition indicates a time when things that should work together are instead at odds. In such times, one cannot expect large undertakings to succeed. But in small matters, by carefully adapting to circumstances while maintaining one's own nature, progress can be made.

lineTexts.text: Remorse disappears. If you lose your horse, do not run after it; it will come back of its own accord. When you see evil people, guard yourself against mistakes.
lineTexts.interpretation: Let go of what has departed; it will return naturally. When encountering difficult people, maintain composure and avoid provocation.

lineTexts.text: One meets his lord in a narrow street. No blame.
lineTexts.interpretation: An informal, chance meeting brings connection despite formal opposition. Unplanned encounters can resolve standoffs.

lineTexts.text: One sees the wagon dragged back, the oxen halted, a man's hair and nose cut off. Not a good beginning, but a good end.
lineTexts.interpretation: Everything seems to go wrong and one appears humiliated. But these obstacles clear the way for eventual success.

lineTexts.text: Isolated through opposition, one meets a like-minded man with whom one can associate in good faith. Despite the danger, no blame.
lineTexts.interpretation: Though isolated by general opposition, finding one trustworthy ally allows progress. This genuine connection brings no blame.

lineTexts.text: Remorse disappears. The companion bites his way through the wrappings. If one goes to him, how could it be a mistake?
lineTexts.interpretation: A loyal friend breaks through barriers to reach you. Going to meet such genuine support is never a mistake.

lineTexts.text: Isolated through opposition. One sees one's companion as a pig covered with dirt, as a wagon full of devils. First one draws a bow against him, then one lays the bow aside. He is not a robber; he will woo at the right time. As one goes, rain falls; then good fortune comes.
lineTexts.interpretation: Suspicion and mistrust create false impressions. When one sees clearly that the perceived enemy is actually a friend, reconciliation brings good fortune like rain after drought.

judgment:
OBSTRUCTION. The southwest furthers.
The northeast does not further.
It furthers one to see the great man.
Perseverance brings good fortune.
image:
Water on the mountain:
The image of OBSTRUCTION.
Thus the superior man turns his attention to himself
And molds his character.
commentary:
The hexagram pictures a dangerous abyss lying before us and a steep, inaccessible mountain rising behind us. We are surrounded by obstacles. The southwest—the direction of retreat—offers safety; the northeast—the direction of advance—does not.

In such a situation, the wise person pauses to examine themselves and cultivate their character. The only response to external obstruction is internal development. When the way forward is blocked, improve yourself while waiting for conditions to change.

lineTexts.text: Going leads to obstructions; coming meets with praise.
lineTexts.interpretation: Attempting to advance brings difficulties; returning brings recognition. This is not the time to push forward.

lineTexts.text: The king's servant is beset by obstruction upon obstruction, but it is not his own fault.
lineTexts.interpretation: One serving a higher cause encounters repeated obstacles. Though difficult, this is no personal failure; duty requires perseverance.

lineTexts.text: Going leads to obstructions; hence he comes back.
lineTexts.interpretation: Recognizing that advancement is impossible, one wisely returns. Those who depend on you welcome your return.

lineTexts.text: Going leads to obstructions; coming leads to union.
lineTexts.interpretation: Advancement alone is blocked, but returning brings connection with others. Together, what was impossible alone becomes possible.

lineTexts.text: In the midst of the greatest obstructions, friends come.
lineTexts.interpretation: At the height of difficulty, help arrives. The obstruction itself draws support from those who recognize the struggle.

lineTexts.text: Going leads to obstructions, coming leads to great good fortune. It furthers one to see the great man.
lineTexts.interpretation: The obstruction reaches its peak and begins to dissolve. Seeking guidance from those of wisdom and experience brings great good fortune.

judgment:
DELIVERANCE. The southwest furthers.
If there is no longer anything where one has to go,
Return brings good fortune.
If there is still something where one has to go,
Hastening brings good fortune.
image:
Thunder and rain set in:
The image of DELIVERANCE.
Thus the superior man pardons mistakes
And forgives misdeeds.
commentary:
This hexagram points to the clearing of tension, the release of difficulties. The relief comes like a thunderstorm that clears the air and brings release after a period of oppressive tension.

Deliverance requires appropriate action. If problems have been fully resolved, return to normal life immediately—don't linger in the crisis mode. If issues remain, address them quickly and decisively. Either way, act in harmony with the moment of release.

lineTexts.text: Without blame.
lineTexts.interpretation: The moment of deliverance has arrived. Nothing needs to be done; simply accepting the release brings no blame.

lineTexts.text: One kills three foxes in the field and receives a yellow arrow. Perseverance brings good fortune.
lineTexts.interpretation: In the process of deliverance, obstacles are removed. Recognition comes for dealing with devious problems. Steadfastness brings good fortune.

lineTexts.text: If a man carries a burden on his back and nonetheless rides in a carriage, he thereby encourages robbers to draw near. Perseverance leads to humiliation.
lineTexts.interpretation: Acting above one's station invites trouble. Clinging to gains beyond one's proper position, even persistently, leads to humiliation.

lineTexts.text: Deliver yourself from your great toe. Then the companion comes, and him you can trust.
lineTexts.interpretation: Free yourself from unworthy attachments. Only then can true friends approach. Remove what hinders genuine connection.

lineTexts.text: If only the superior man can deliver himself, it brings good fortune. Thus he proves to inferior men that he is in earnest.
lineTexts.interpretation: The capable person extricates themselves from difficulty. This self-deliverance demonstrates authentic character to all.

lineTexts.text: The prince shoots at a hawk on a high wall. He kills it. Everything serves to further.
lineTexts.interpretation: The final obstacle is overcome through skill and decisiveness. Removing the last barrier to deliverance brings complete success.

judgment:
DECREASE combined with sincerity
Brings about supreme good fortune
Without blame.
One may be persevering in this.
It furthers one to undertake something.
How is this to be carried out?
One may use two small bowls for the sacrifice.
image:
At the foot of the mountain, the lake:
The image of DECREASE.
Thus the superior man controls his anger
And restrains his instincts.
commentary:
Decrease does not under all circumstances mean something bad. Increase and decrease come in their own time. What matters is to understand the time and not to cover up poverty with empty pretense.

When the lake at the foot of the mountain evaporates, the mountain is enriched by the moisture. The lower gives to the higher. This brings sacrifice but also opportunity for refinement. When decrease is based on inner truth, the simplest offerings suffice.

lineTexts.text: Going quickly when one's tasks are finished is without blame. But one must reflect on how much one may decrease others.
lineTexts.interpretation: Completing duties promptly is good. But consider how your efficiency may burden those who must now carry more.

lineTexts.text: Perseverance furthers. To undertake something brings misfortune. Without decreasing oneself, one is able to bring increase to others.
lineTexts.interpretation: Maintain constancy but avoid new ventures. Help others without depleting yourself; sustainable giving is better than sacrifice.

lineTexts.text: When three people journey together, their number decreases by one. When one man journeys alone, he finds a companion.
lineTexts.interpretation: Three is an unstable number; one naturally drops off. But one alone naturally finds a companion. Decrease leads to appropriate union.

lineTexts.text: If a man decreases his faults, it makes the other hasten to come and rejoice. No blame.
lineTexts.interpretation: Reducing one's own faults attracts others quickly and brings joy. Self-improvement draws support without blame.

lineTexts.text: Someone does indeed increase him. Ten pairs of tortoises cannot oppose it. Supreme good fortune.
lineTexts.interpretation: Unexpected enrichment comes; nothing can prevent this blessing. When fate brings increase, even oracles cannot countermand it.

lineTexts.text: If one is increased without depriving others, there is no blame. Perseverance brings good fortune. It furthers one to undertake something. One obtains servants but no longer has a separate home.
lineTexts.interpretation: Increase that harms no one is without blame. Such gain leads to greater responsibilities and a larger sphere of influence.

judgment:
INCREASE. It furthers one
To undertake something.
It furthers one to cross the great water.
image:
Wind and thunder: the image of INCREASE.
Thus the superior man:
If he sees good, he imitates it;
If he has faults, he rids himself of them.
commentary:
The idea of increase is expressed in the fact that the strong lowest line of the upper trigram has sunk down and taken its place under the lower trigram. This represents sacrifice from above that reaches the people and furthers their development.

This is a time favorable for all kinds of undertakings. The time is propitious; benefit flows downward, creating goodwill and harmony. One should use this time to accomplish something great.

lineTexts.text: It furthers one to accomplish great deeds. Supreme good fortune. No blame.
lineTexts.interpretation: The time is ideal for significant undertakings. The support from above makes great accomplishment possible without blame.

lineTexts.text: Someone does indeed increase him; ten pairs of tortoises cannot oppose it. Constant perseverance brings good fortune. The king presents him before God. Good fortune.
lineTexts.interpretation: Destined blessings come that cannot be refused. Persistence brings good fortune, and one's merit is recognized at the highest level.

lineTexts.text: One is enriched through unfortunate events. No blame, if you are sincere and walk in the middle, and report to the prince with a seal.
lineTexts.interpretation: Benefit comes through difficult circumstances. If one acts sincerely and officially reports the situation, there is no blame.

lineTexts.text: If you walk in the middle and report to the prince, he will follow. It furthers one to be used in the removal of the capital.
lineTexts.interpretation: One in a mediating position can influence decisions above. This is the time to propose major changes; they will be supported.

lineTexts.text: If in truth you have a kind heart, ask not. Supreme good fortune. Truly, kindness will be recognized as your virtue.
lineTexts.interpretation: Genuine benevolence needs no proof or questioning. Supreme good fortune comes to one whose kindness is authentic.

lineTexts.text: He brings increase to no one. Indeed, someone even strikes him. He does not keep his heart constantly steady. Misfortune.
lineTexts.interpretation: One who fails to share increase with others loses it all. Inconstancy of heart brings misfortune; generosity must be genuine and sustained.

judgment:
BREAK-THROUGH. One must resolutely make the matter known
At the court of the king.
It must be announced truthfully. Danger.
It is necessary to notify one's own city.
It does not further to resort to arms.
It furthers one to undertake something.
image:
The lake has risen up to heaven:
The image of BREAK-THROUGH.
Thus the superior man
Dispenses riches downward
And refrains from resting on his virtue.
commentary:
This hexagram signifies a break-through after a long accumulation of tension, as a swollen river breaks through its dikes. The five strong lines are about to force out the one weak line at the top. Decisive action is indicated.

But the action must be appropriate: open declaration of the truth, warning to allies, and then resolute forward movement. Direct force is not the way; the weak yields to consistent pressure without violence.

lineTexts.text: Mighty in the forward-striding toes. When one goes and is not equal to the task, one makes a mistake.
lineTexts.interpretation: Rushing forward eagerly without adequate strength leads to failure. Assess your capabilities before acting.

lineTexts.text: A cry of alarm. Arms at evening and at night. Fear nothing.
lineTexts.interpretation: Vigilance at all hours. Though danger threatens, maintaining alertness removes fear. Preparedness dispels anxiety.

lineTexts.text: To be powerful in the cheekbones brings misfortune. The superior man is firmly resolved. He walks alone and is caught in the rain. He is bespattered, and people murmur against him. No blame.
lineTexts.interpretation: Aggressive expression brings misfortune. The resolute person proceeds alone despite criticism and discomfort; ultimately there is no blame.

lineTexts.text: There is no skin on his thighs, and walking comes hard. If a man were to let himself be led like a sheep, remorse would disappear. But if these words are heard, they will not be believed.
lineTexts.interpretation: Progress is painful and difficult. Following guidance would help, but such advice is not heeded. A warning likely to be ignored.

lineTexts.text: In dealing with weeds, firm resolution is necessary. Walking in the middle remains free of blame.
lineTexts.interpretation: Persistent problems require determined action. Maintaining the middle way while being resolute brings no blame.

lineTexts.text: No cry. In the end misfortune comes.
lineTexts.interpretation: Failing to sound the alarm or take action when needed leads to misfortune. Silence when speech is called for is a failure.

judgment:
COMING TO MEET. The maiden is powerful.
One should not marry such a maiden.
image:
Under heaven, wind:
The image of COMING TO MEET.
Thus does the prince act when disseminating his commands
And proclaiming them to the four quarters of heaven.
commentary:
This hexagram indicates a situation where the dark principle, represented by a yielding line, has risen unexpectedly from below and is just about to push upward. The situation must be understood in its incipient stage.

A strong reaction against something dark and corrupting is necessary. The dark force appears harmless at first but can grow quickly in power if not checked. Recognition and immediate action are required.

lineTexts.text: It must be checked with a brake of bronze. Perseverance brings good fortune. If one lets it take its course, one experiences misfortune. Even a lean pig has it in him to rage around.
lineTexts.interpretation: The corrupting influence must be firmly restrained from the start. What seems weak can become dangerously powerful if not checked.

lineTexts.text: There is a fish in the tank. No blame. Does not further guests.
lineTexts.interpretation: The dark element is contained and controlled. Keep it under wraps; do not let it spread to others.

lineTexts.text: There is no skin on his thighs, and walking comes hard. If one is mindful of the danger, no great mistake is made.
lineTexts.interpretation: Movement is difficult and painful. Awareness of the difficulty prevents major errors. Proceed with caution.

lineTexts.text: No fish in the tank. This leads to misfortune.
lineTexts.interpretation: The people have been alienated; connection has been lost. Without this bond, misfortune follows.

lineTexts.text: A melon covered with willow leaves. Hidden lines. Then it drops down to one from heaven.
lineTexts.interpretation: Inner worth concealed under modest appearance. Patiently maintaining integrity brings unexpected blessing from above.

lineTexts.text: He comes to meet with his horns. Humiliation. No blame.
lineTexts.interpretation: Meeting others with aggressive resistance brings humiliation. Though understandable, such defensiveness is regrettable.

judgment:
GATHERING TOGETHER. Success.
The king approaches his temple.
It furthers one to see the great man.
This brings success. Perseverance furthers.
To bring great offerings creates good fortune.
It furthers one to undertake something.
image:
Over the earth, the lake:
The image of GATHERING TOGETHER.
Thus the superior man renews his weapons
In order to meet the unforeseen.
commentary:
This hexagram depicts a gathering of people around a leader. The lake above the earth suggests a congregation, like water gathering in a lake. Such a gathering requires a center—a leader or purpose—around which people can unite.

The time is favorable for coming together, but preparation is needed. Gatherings can be dangerous if not properly organized. Great offerings suggest the spirit of sacrifice necessary for successful collective action.

lineTexts.text: If you are sincere, but not to the end, there will sometimes be confusion, sometimes gathering together. If you call out, then after one grasp of the hand you can laugh again. Regret not. Going is without blame.
lineTexts.interpretation: Wavering commitment causes confusion. But genuine connection can be restored quickly. Don't hesitate to reach out.

lineTexts.text: Letting oneself be drawn brings good fortune and remains blameless. If one is sincere, it furthers one to bring even a small offering.
lineTexts.interpretation: Allow yourself to be drawn into the gathering. Even a modest contribution, sincerely given, is valuable.

lineTexts.text: Gathering together amid sighs. Nothing that would further. Going is without blame. Slight humiliation.
lineTexts.interpretation: Wanting to join but finding no connection brings sighs. Proceeding anyway causes slight embarrassment but no real harm.

lineTexts.text: Great good fortune. No blame.
lineTexts.interpretation: One gathers people around not for personal gain but in service to the leader. This brings great good fortune and no blame.

lineTexts.text: If in gathering together one has position, this brings no blame. If there are some who are not yet sincerely in the work, sublime and enduring perseverance is needed. Then remorse disappears.
lineTexts.interpretation: Leading the gathering with legitimate authority. If some remain unconvinced, patient persistence wins them over.

lineTexts.text: Lamenting and sighing, floods of tears. No blame.
lineTexts.interpretation: Being unable to join the gathering causes deep sorrow. Such genuine grief at exclusion brings no blame.

judgment:
PUSHING UPWARD has supreme success.
One must see the great man.
Fear not.
Departure toward the south
Brings good fortune.
image:
Within the earth, wood grows:
The image of PUSHING UPWARD.
Thus the superior man of devoted character
Heaps up small things
In order to achieve something high and great.
commentary:
The lower trigram, Sun, represents wood; the upper, K'un, represents earth. Wood grows within the earth and works its way up to the light. This pushing upward suggests the effort of rising step by step through devoted work.

Unlike sudden advancement, this is gradual progress through diligent effort. Small accumulations over time lead to great results. The effort requires adaptability and persistence, like a plant that grows toward the light.

lineTexts.text: Pushing upward that meets with confidence brings great good fortune.
lineTexts.interpretation: The beginning of upward movement is welcomed and trusted. This confident support enables great good fortune.

lineTexts.text: If one is sincere, it furthers one to bring even a small offering. No blame.
lineTexts.interpretation: Genuine sincerity matters more than grand gestures. Even modest contributions, if sincere, bring no blame.

lineTexts.text: One pushes upward into an empty city.
lineTexts.interpretation: Advancement meets no resistance. The path forward is clear and unobstructed. Progress is easy.

lineTexts.text: The king offers him Mount Ch'i. Good fortune. No blame.
lineTexts.interpretation: Recognition comes from the highest authority. The upward journey receives official blessing and support.

lineTexts.text: Perseverance brings good fortune. One pushes upward by steps.
lineTexts.interpretation: Steady, step-by-step advancement brings good fortune. Don't rush; consistent progress is more reliable than leaps.

lineTexts.text: Pushing upward in darkness. It furthers one to be unremittingly persevering.
lineTexts.interpretation: At the height of the climb, visibility is lost. Only unwavering persistence prevents a fall. Keep going despite uncertainty.

judgment:
OPPRESSION. Success. Perseverance.
The great man brings about good fortune.
No blame.
When one has something to say,
It is not believed.
image:
There is no water in the lake:
The image of EXHAUSTION.
Thus the superior man stakes his life
On following his will.
commentary:
The lake is above, water below; the lake is empty, drained away. Exhaustion of resources is indicated. This is a time of adversity when one's words are not believed and actions are blocked.

Yet success remains possible for the great person who maintains inner constancy. The exhaustion is of external resources, not inner character. One must draw on inner reserves and wait for conditions to improve. True character shows itself in adversity.

lineTexts.text: One sits oppressed under a bare tree and strays into a gloomy valley. For three years one sees nothing.
lineTexts.interpretation: Deep depression and confusion. One has lost the way and cannot find it. This period of darkness lasts a long time.

lineTexts.text: One is oppressed while at meat and drink. The man with the scarlet knee bands is just coming. It furthers one to offer sacrifice. To set forth brings misfortune. No blame.
lineTexts.interpretation: Oppression in the midst of comfort. Help is approaching. Make spiritual preparations rather than external action.

lineTexts.text: A man permits himself to be oppressed by stone and leans on thorns and thistles. He enters his house and does not see his wife. Misfortune.
lineTexts.interpretation: Oppressed by rigid external circumstances and finding no comfort at home. Complete isolation and misfortune.

lineTexts.text: He comes very quietly, oppressed in a golden carriage. Humiliation, but the end is reached.
lineTexts.interpretation: One in a privileged position is still oppressed, approaching carefully and somewhat shamefully. But the difficulty reaches its end.

lineTexts.text: His nose and feet are cut off. Oppression at the hands of the man with the purple knee bands. Joy comes softly. It furthers one to make offerings and libations.
lineTexts.interpretation: Severe oppression from those in authority. Yet gentle joy approaches. Spiritual practices bring relief.

lineTexts.text: He is oppressed by creeping vines. He moves uncertainly and says, 'Movement brings remorse.' If one feels remorse over this and makes a start, good fortune comes.
lineTexts.interpretation: Entangled in difficulties and hesitant to move. But recognizing this paralysis and making a fresh start brings good fortune.

judgment:
THE WELL. The town may be changed,
But the well cannot be changed.
It neither decreases nor increases.
They come and go and draw from the well.
If one gets down almost to the water
And the rope does not go all the way,
Or the jug breaks, it brings misfortune.
image:
Water over wood: the image of THE WELL.
Thus the superior man encourages the people at their work,
And exhorts them to help one another.
commentary:
The well is the source of life for a community. It remains constant while everything else changes. Cities may move, populations may shift, but the wells remain where the water is.

This hexagram refers to the inexhaustible source of nourishment, both physical and spiritual. But the well must be properly maintained and used. If the rope is too short or the vessel breaks, the water cannot be drawn—the resource is there but cannot be accessed. Care and diligence are required.

lineTexts.text: One does not drink the mud of the well. No animals come to an old well.
lineTexts.interpretation: A neglected well becomes unusable. If the source of nourishment is not maintained, no one benefits from it.

lineTexts.text: At the well hole one shoots fishes. The jug is broken and leaks.
lineTexts.interpretation: The well has good water but is being misused. The vessel for drawing is damaged. Capabilities exist but are wasted.

lineTexts.text: The well is cleaned, but no one drinks from it. This is my heart's sorrow, for one might draw from it. If the king were clear-minded, good fortune might be enjoyed in common.
lineTexts.interpretation: The source is pure and ready, but no one uses it. This is a tragedy. If those in authority recognized its value, everyone would benefit.

lineTexts.text: The well is being lined. No blame.
lineTexts.interpretation: The well is being repaired and maintained. No water can be drawn during renovation, but this maintenance brings no blame.

lineTexts.text: In the well there is a clear, cold spring from which one can drink.
lineTexts.interpretation: The well functions perfectly. The source is pure and accessible. Genuine nourishment is available for all.

lineTexts.text: One draws from the well without hindrance. It is dependable. Supreme good fortune.
lineTexts.interpretation: The well serves freely and reliably. Nothing blocks access to its inexhaustible nourishment. Supreme good fortune for all who draw from it.

judgment:
REVOLUTION. On your own day
You are believed.
Supreme success,
Furthering through perseverance.
Remorse disappears.
image:
Fire in the lake: the image of REVOLUTION.
Thus the superior man
Sets the calendar in order
And makes the seasons clear.
commentary:
Fire below and lake above combat each other. The lake tries to extinguish the fire, the fire tries to evaporate the lake. Hence the idea of revolution, of one thing taking the place of another.

Revolution requires the right time, genuine preparation, and proper execution. Only when these conditions are met will the revolution be believed and supported. Revolutionary change must emerge from necessity, not mere desire for novelty.

lineTexts.text: Wrapped in the hide of a yellow cow.
lineTexts.interpretation: It is not yet time for revolution. One should remain still and prepare, binding oneself to patience and proper form.

lineTexts.text: When one's own day comes, one may create revolution. Starting brings good fortune. No blame.
lineTexts.interpretation: When the right time arrives, revolution may proceed. Beginning the transformation at the proper moment brings good fortune.

lineTexts.text: Starting brings misfortune. Perseverance brings danger. When talk of revolution has gone the rounds three times, one may commit himself, and men will believe him.
lineTexts.interpretation: Premature action brings misfortune. Wait until the case for change has been thoroughly discussed and understood before acting.

lineTexts.text: Remorse disappears. Men believe him. Changing the form of government brings good fortune.
lineTexts.interpretation: Regret vanishes as trust is established. The time is right for fundamental institutional change.

lineTexts.text: The great man changes like a tiger. Even before he questions the oracle he is believed.
lineTexts.interpretation: The great person transforms boldly and magnificently, like a tiger revealing its stripes. Such authentic change inspires immediate trust.

lineTexts.text: The superior man changes like a panther. The inferior man molts in the face. Starting brings misfortune. To remain persevering brings good fortune.
lineTexts.interpretation: The noble person transforms elegantly and completely. Lesser people change only superficially. After revolution is complete, no new starts should be made.

judgment:
THE CALDRON. Supreme good fortune.
Success.
image:
Fire over wood:
The image of THE CALDRON.
Thus the superior man consolidates his fate
By making his position correct.
commentary:
The caldron is the symbol of nourishment and of sacrifice. It is the image of a civilization that has reached its highest form. Wood feeds fire, which transforms raw food into nourishment—a profound symbol of cultural transformation.

The caldron also represents the vessel of the sage, the container of wisdom that nourishes the people. Like a great vessel properly positioned over fire, the noble person establishes themselves correctly to serve their function in society.

lineTexts.text: A ting with legs upturned. Furthers removal of stagnating stuff. One takes a concubine for the sake of her son. No blame.
lineTexts.interpretation: The vessel is overturned to clean it. Removing what is stale makes room for renewal. Unconventional means may serve worthy ends.

lineTexts.text: There is food in the ting. My comrades are envious, but they cannot harm me. Good fortune.
lineTexts.interpretation: Genuine substance attracts envy, but inner worth protects one from harm. Good fortune comes through authentic nourishment.

lineTexts.text: The handle of the ting is altered. One is impeded in his way of life. The fat of the pheasant is not eaten. Once rain falls, remorse is spent. Good fortune comes in the end.
lineTexts.interpretation: The vessel cannot be properly lifted; access is blocked. But cleansing rains come, washing away obstacles. Eventually, good fortune.

lineTexts.text: The legs of the ting are broken. The prince's meal is spilled and his person soiled. Misfortune.
lineTexts.interpretation: The vessel fails under its burden. Valuable nourishment is lost and the one responsible is disgraced. Misfortune through inadequacy.

lineTexts.text: The ting has yellow handles, golden carrying rings. Perseverance furthers.
lineTexts.interpretation: The vessel has proper handles and rings—the means of use are excellent. The inner content can be properly delivered. Perseverance furthers.

lineTexts.text: The ting has rings of jade. Great good fortune. Nothing that would not act to further.
lineTexts.interpretation: The vessel has reached perfection. All its parts work in harmony. Supreme good fortune in all undertakings.

judgment:
SHOCK brings success.
Shock comes—oh, oh!
Laughing words—ha, ha!
The shock terrifies for a hundred miles,
And he does not let fall the sacrificial spoon and chalice.
image:
Thunder repeated: the image of SHOCK.
Thus in fear and trembling
The superior man sets his life in order
And examines himself.
commentary:
The hexagram represents thunder doubled, the firstborn son. Thunder brings shock and fear, but also awakening and renewal. The shock that comes suddenly tests one's composure.

The image of one who does not drop the sacrificial implements despite being terrified for a hundred miles shows maintaining inner composure through the greatest shocks. Such presence of mind comes from inner cultivation and leads to success.

lineTexts.text: Shock comes—oh, oh! Then follow laughing words—ha, ha! Good fortune.
lineTexts.interpretation: The first shock terrifies, but recovery brings laughter. Initial fear gives way to joy when one realizes all is well.

lineTexts.text: Shock comes bringing danger. A hundred thousand times you lose your treasures and must climb the nine hills. Do not go in pursuit of them. After seven days you will get them back again.
lineTexts.interpretation: Great shock brings loss. Do not chase what is gone; it will return naturally. Accept temporary loss with equanimity.

lineTexts.text: Shock comes and makes one distraught. If shock spurs to action, one remains free of misfortune.
lineTexts.interpretation: Shock disturbs and unsettles. But if this agitation leads to constructive action rather than paralysis, misfortune is avoided.

lineTexts.text: Shock is mired.
lineTexts.interpretation: The shock sinks into mud; energy is dissipated. One cannot move forward or back. Paralysis in the face of shock.

lineTexts.text: Shock goes hither and thither. Danger. However, nothing at all is lost. Yet there are things to be done.
lineTexts.interpretation: Repeated shocks threaten from all directions. Though nothing is ultimately lost, there is work to be done amid the turmoil.

lineTexts.text: Shock brings ruin and terrified gazing around. Going ahead brings misfortune. If it has not yet touched one's own body, but has reached one's neighbor first, there is no blame. One's comrades have something to talk about.
lineTexts.interpretation: Shock has reached its extreme. Do not advance. If the worst has struck others first, take warning without blame. Learn from neighbors' misfortune.

judgment:
KEEPING STILL. Keeping his back still
So that he no longer feels his body.
He goes into his courtyard
And does not see his people.
No blame.
image:
Mountains standing close together:
The image of KEEPING STILL.
Thus the superior man
Does not permit his thoughts
To go beyond his situation.
commentary:
True quiet means keeping still when the time has come to keep still, and going forward when the time has come to go forward. In this way rest and movement are in agreement with the demands of the time, and thus there is light in life.

The heart thinks constantly. This cannot be changed. But the movements of the heart—that is, the thoughts—should restrict themselves to the immediate situation. All thinking that goes beyond this only makes the heart sore.

lineTexts.text: Keeping his toes still. No blame. Continued perseverance furthers.
lineTexts.interpretation: Stillness at the very beginning, before movement starts. Remaining still from the outset prevents error and supports perseverance.

lineTexts.text: Keeping his calves still. He cannot rescue him whom he follows. His heart is not glad.
lineTexts.interpretation: One is forced to remain still though wanting to follow another. Unable to help, the heart is troubled, but nothing can be done.

lineTexts.text: Keeping his hips still. Making his sacrum stiff. Dangerous. The heart suffocates.
lineTexts.interpretation: Forced stillness in the wrong place—at the hips where motion originates. This unnatural rigidity is dangerous and stifling.

lineTexts.text: Keeping his trunk still. No blame.
lineTexts.interpretation: The natural center of stillness. Keeping the trunk still while allowing appropriate movement of limbs is correct. No blame.

lineTexts.text: Keeping his jaws still. The words have order. Remorse disappears.
lineTexts.interpretation: Stillness of speech—speaking only when necessary, with proper order and measure. This discipline removes regret.

lineTexts.text: Noble-hearted keeping still. Good fortune.
lineTexts.interpretation: Complete, wholehearted stillness from deep understanding. This genuine tranquility brings good fortune.

judgment:
DEVELOPMENT. The maiden
Is given in marriage.
Good fortune.
Perseverance furthers.
image:
On the mountain, a tree:
The image of DEVELOPMENT.
Thus the superior man abides in dignity and virtue,
In order to improve the mores.
commentary:
The development of events that leads to marriage is compared to the gradual flight of wild geese. They fly in proper order, maintaining their ranks. So too should development in life proceed—step by step, following proper form.

A tree on a mountain develops slowly but surely, adapting to its position. This gradual, organic growth represents the proper way for lasting development. Hasty growth cannot endure; slow, steady progress creates lasting results.

lineTexts.text: The wild goose gradually draws near the shore. The young son is in danger. There is talk. No blame.
lineTexts.interpretation: The beginning of development. A young person faces criticism and some danger. But this is the normal process of beginning; no blame.

lineTexts.text: The wild goose gradually draws near the cliff. Eating and drinking in peace and concord. Good fortune.
lineTexts.interpretation: A safe resting place has been reached. Enjoying fellowship and nourishment in peace. Good fortune through contentment.

lineTexts.text: The wild goose gradually draws near the plateau. The man goes forth and does not return. The woman carries a child but does not bring it forth. Misfortune. It furthers one to fight off robbers.
lineTexts.interpretation: Progress has led to danger. Premature advancement brings loss and failed development. Defense against those who would exploit this weakness is necessary.

lineTexts.text: The wild goose gradually draws near the tree. Perhaps it will find a flat branch. No blame.
lineTexts.interpretation: An unusual position—geese don't normally perch in trees. But adaptability finds a workable solution. No blame for making the best of circumstances.

lineTexts.text: The wild goose gradually draws near the summit. For three years the woman has no child. In the end nothing can hinder her. Good fortune.
lineTexts.interpretation: After long separation, union is achieved. Delays and obstacles eventually yield. Perseverance through the long wait brings good fortune.

lineTexts.text: The wild goose gradually draws near the cloud heights. Its feathers can be used for the sacred dance. Good fortune.
lineTexts.interpretation: The highest development is reached. What has been achieved becomes an inspiration and offering. Supreme good fortune.

judgment:
THE MARRYING MAIDEN.
Undertakings bring misfortune.
Nothing that would further.
image:
Thunder over the lake:
The image of THE MARRYING MAIDEN.
Thus the superior man
Understands the transitory
In the light of the eternity of the end.
commentary:
This hexagram represents the relationship of an older man with a young, secondary woman—a situation of natural but subordinate position. The essential thing is to maintain proper decorum in such relationships.

The trigram of arousal (Thunder) above and of joy (Lake) below suggests emotional attraction not bound by proper form. This leads to difficulties unless carefully managed. Understanding the eternal perspective helps navigate transitory situations.

lineTexts.text: The marrying maiden as a concubine. A lame man who is able to tread. Undertakings bring good fortune.
lineTexts.interpretation: A secondary position accepted with grace. Like one who can still walk despite lameness, one can still function effectively despite limitations. Good fortune in undertakings.

lineTexts.text: A one-eyed man who is able to see. The perseverance of a solitary man furthers.
lineTexts.interpretation: Reduced capacity, but still functional. Maintaining constancy and independence despite diminished circumstances furthers progress.

lineTexts.text: The marrying maiden as a slave. She marries as a concubine.
lineTexts.interpretation: One who aspires too high ends up lower than expected. Ambition beyond one's true position results in a subordinate role.

lineTexts.text: The marrying maiden draws out the allotted time. A late marriage comes in due course.
lineTexts.interpretation: Waiting beyond the expected time. Delay is preferable to an unsuitable match. The right union comes eventually.

lineTexts.text: The sovereign I gave his daughter in marriage. The embroidered garments of the princess were not as gorgeous as those of the servingmaid. The moon that is nearly full brings good fortune.
lineTexts.interpretation: True worth lies in inner quality, not outward display. Modesty in appearance despite high position brings good fortune, like the moon just before it becomes full.

lineTexts.text: The woman holds the basket, but there are no fruits in it. The man stabs the sheep, but no blood flows. Nothing that acts to further.
lineTexts.interpretation: Empty forms without substance. The ritual is performed but nothing real is accomplished. No benefit comes from hollow actions.

judgment:
ABUNDANCE has success.
The king attains abundance.
Be not sad.
Be like the sun at midday.
image:
Both thunder and lightning come:
The image of ABUNDANCE.
Thus the superior man decides lawsuits
And carries out punishments.
commentary:
This hexagram represents a time of peak abundance, when both clarity and movement combine at their maximum. Like the sun at noon, this fullness cannot last—it must begin to decline.

The point is not to grieve about the impermanence of abundance, but to use it fully while it lasts. Be like the sun at midday: shine at full power without reservation, knowing that this is the nature of fullness. Act with clarity and energy while the time is favorable.

lineTexts.text: When a man meets his destined ruler, they can be together ten days, and it is not a mistake. Going meets with recognition.
lineTexts.interpretation: Meeting one's true counterpart at the time of abundance. Extended engagement is appropriate. Actions are recognized and valued.

lineTexts.text: The curtain is of such fullness that the polestars can be seen at noon. Through going one meets with mistrust and hate. If one rouses him through truth, good fortune comes.
lineTexts.interpretation: Though it is midday, darkness seems to fall. One encounters suspicion. But arousing others through genuine truth brings good fortune.

lineTexts.text: The underbrush is of such abundance that the small stars can be seen at noon. He breaks his right arm. No blame.
lineTexts.interpretation: The darkness has thickened further. Action is impaired, as if one's right arm were broken. But this is no one's fault—it's the nature of the time.

lineTexts.text: The curtain is of such fullness that the polestars can be seen at noon. He meets his ruler, who is of like kind. Good fortune.
lineTexts.interpretation: Though darkness seems to prevail, one finds a kindred spirit with whom to work. This meeting of like minds brings good fortune.

lineTexts.text: Lines are coming. Blessing and fame draw near. Good fortune.
lineTexts.interpretation: Opening to capable people brings blessing. Those of brilliance approach and are welcomed. Fame and good fortune follow.

lineTexts.text: His house is in a state of abundance. He screens off his family. He peers through the gate and no longer perceives anyone. For three years he sees nothing. Misfortune.
lineTexts.interpretation: Abundance has led to isolation. One has shut oneself away from others. This self-imposed solitude brings misfortune.

judgment:
THE WANDERER. Success through smallness.
Perseverance brings good fortune to the wanderer.
image:
Fire on the mountain:
The image of THE WANDERER.
Thus the superior man
Is clear-minded and cautious
In imposing penalties,
And protracts no lawsuits.
commentary:
A traveler has no home; they must adapt to changing circumstances. Fire on a mountain flares up quickly but cannot last—it must move on. This is the condition of the wanderer: transience is fundamental.

The wanderer must be careful and modest. Without the security of an established position, one must rely on inner clarity and caution. Small successes are possible, but not great undertakings. Perseverance in the right conduct brings good fortune even in this uncertain state.

lineTexts.text: If the wanderer busies himself with trivial things, he draws down misfortune upon himself.
lineTexts.interpretation: The wanderer who gets entangled in petty matters invites trouble. Maintain dignity and focus; don't stoop to small concerns.

lineTexts.text: The wanderer comes to an inn. He has his property with him. He wins the steadfastness of a young servant.
lineTexts.interpretation: Finding temporary shelter with resources intact. Gaining a loyal helper. The wanderer manages well through caution and propriety.

lineTexts.text: The wanderer's inn burns down. He loses the steadfastness of his young servant. Danger.
lineTexts.interpretation: Loss of shelter and loss of support. The wanderer's precarious position is exposed. Danger from lack of proper conduct.

lineTexts.text: The wanderer rests in a shelter. He obtains his property and an ax. My heart is not glad.
lineTexts.interpretation: A resting place and resources are found, including means of protection. But the heart remains unsatisfied—this is not home.

lineTexts.text: He shoots a pheasant. It drops with the first arrow. In the end this brings both praise and office.
lineTexts.interpretation: Success in a worthy endeavor while traveling. Skills demonstrated effectively lead to recognition and position.

lineTexts.text: The bird's nest burns up. The wanderer laughs first, then must needs lament and weep. Through carelessness he loses his cow. Misfortune.
lineTexts.interpretation: The wanderer forgets their position and becomes careless. Initial enjoyment gives way to loss and sorrow. Misfortune through negligence.

judgment:
THE GENTLE. Success through what is small.
It furthers one to have somewhere to go.
It furthers one to see the great man.
image:
Winds following one upon the other:
The image of THE GENTLY PENETRATING.
Thus the superior man
Spreads his commands abroad
And carries out his undertakings.
commentary:
The trigram Sun represents the eldest daughter, the gentle wind that penetrates everywhere. Unlike the forceful impact of a single strong push, the gentle produces effects through continuous influence.

Penetration is achieved not by force but by steady, persistent influence. Like wind that works its way into every crack, gentle perseverance accomplishes what direct confrontation cannot. The hexagram counsels patience and consistent effort toward clear goals.

lineTexts.text: In advancing and in retreating, the perseverance of a warrior furthers.
lineTexts.interpretation: Whether going forward or back, maintain the discipline and resolution of a warrior. Decisive action prevents aimless wavering.

lineTexts.text: Penetration under the bed. Priests and magicians are used in great number. Good fortune. No blame.
lineTexts.interpretation: Seeking out hidden influences through many means and helpers. Thorough investigation through various approaches brings good fortune.

lineTexts.text: Repeated penetration. Humiliation.
lineTexts.interpretation: Excessive analysis and repeated attempts at the same problem lead to humiliation. Know when to stop probing.

lineTexts.text: Remorse vanishes. During the hunt three kinds of game are caught.
lineTexts.interpretation: Regret disappears as success comes. Multiple goals are achieved through proper approach. Effective action brings varied results.

lineTexts.text: Perseverance brings good fortune. Remorse vanishes. Nothing that does not further. No beginning, but an end. Before the change, three days. After the change, three days. Good fortune.
lineTexts.interpretation: Steady effort brings good fortune. Though the start was not ideal, the outcome will be. Allow time before and after changes to settle.

lineTexts.text: Penetration under the bed. He loses his property and his ax. Perseverance brings misfortune.
lineTexts.interpretation: Excessive penetration into hidden matters goes too far. Resources and means of action are lost. Continued persistence brings misfortune.

judgment:
THE JOYOUS. Success.
Perseverance is favorable.
image:
Lakes resting one on the other:
The image of THE JOYOUS.
Thus the superior man joins with his friends
For discussion and practice.
commentary:
The trigram Dui means to joy, to talk, to break through. Two lakes joined together represent mutual enrichment and joy. This is the joy that comes from exchange and sharing.

True joy is not mere pleasure but the deep satisfaction that comes from inner harmony and genuine connection with others. Such joy inspires and encourages. The superior person cultivates this joy through discussion and shared practice with like-minded friends.

lineTexts.text: Contented joyousness. Good fortune.
lineTexts.interpretation: Simple, genuine joy that expects nothing. This inner contentment, independent of external conditions, brings good fortune.

lineTexts.text: Sincere joyousness. Good fortune. Remorse disappears.
lineTexts.interpretation: Joy grounded in inner truth and sincerity. Such authentic joy dissolves regret and brings good fortune.

lineTexts.text: Coming joyousness. Misfortune.
lineTexts.interpretation: Joy sought from external sources, coming from outside oneself. Depending on external things for joy leads to misfortune.

lineTexts.text: Joyousness that is weighed is not at peace. After ridding himself of mistakes a man has joy.
lineTexts.interpretation: Deliberating about joy shows inner conflict. Only after eliminating faults can one experience true, untroubled joy.

lineTexts.text: Sincerity toward disintegrating influences is dangerous.
lineTexts.interpretation: Trusting in forces that are actually harmful is dangerous. Discernment is needed about what truly deserves allegiance.

lineTexts.text: Seductive joyousness.
lineTexts.interpretation: The highest form of joy can become seduction, pulling oneself or others toward excess. Awareness of this danger is necessary.

judgment:
DISPERSION. Success.
The king approaches his temple.
It furthers one to cross the great water.
Perseverance furthers.
image:
The wind drives over the water:
The image of DISPERSION.
Thus the kings of old sacrificed to the Lord
And built temples.
commentary:
Wind blowing over water disperses it. Similarly, when a man's vital energy is dammed up within him, it may be dispersed through religious observances and sacred music. Dispersion can dissolve what has become rigid and blocked.

The king approaches the temple to worship, dispersing his own ego and connecting with something greater. This is the positive use of dispersion: breaking up rigidity, dissolving isolation, and reunifying what has been separated.

lineTexts.text: He brings help with the strength of a horse. Good fortune.
lineTexts.interpretation: At the first signs of division, energetic intervention prevents further scattering. Swift, powerful assistance brings good fortune.

lineTexts.text: At the dissolution he hurries to that which supports him. Remorse disappears.
lineTexts.interpretation: When things disperse, one seeks the support that remains constant. Finding solid ground, regret vanishes.

lineTexts.text: He dissolves his self. No remorse.
lineTexts.interpretation: Dissolving one's ego, one's narrow self-interest, brings no regret. Self-transcendence in service of something greater.

lineTexts.text: He dissolves his bond with his group. Supreme good fortune. Dispersion leads in turn to accumulation. This is something that ordinary men do not think of.
lineTexts.interpretation: Breaking from the group to reunite on a higher level. What seems like separation actually leads to greater gathering. Extraordinary insight.

lineTexts.text: His loud cries are as dissolving as sweat. Dissolution—a king abides without blame.
lineTexts.interpretation: Great proclamations disperse doubt and confusion like sweat leaving the body. The leader who guides through crisis maintains authority without blame.

lineTexts.text: He dissolves his blood. Departing, keeping at a distance, going out, is without blame.
lineTexts.interpretation: Dissolving even blood ties—the closest connections—when necessary for protection. Departing to avoid harm brings no blame.

judgment:
LIMITATION. Success.
Galling limitation must not be persevered in.
image:
Water over lake: the image of LIMITATION.
Thus the superior man
Creates number and measure,
And examines the nature of virtue and correct conduct.
commentary:
The lake is limited by the level of water it can hold; if this is exceeded, the water overflows. Limits are necessary, but they must not be too oppressive. A lake must have boundaries to exist, but if confined too tightly, it becomes stagnant.

Limitation that is too severe cannot be maintained and will ultimately be broken. The wise person sets limits that are sustainable and just, neither too loose nor too rigid. Self-imposed limits, when appropriate, create freedom rather than constraining it.

lineTexts.text: Not going out of the door and the courtyard is without blame.
lineTexts.interpretation: Knowing when not to act is itself a form of wisdom. Limiting oneself appropriately at the start prevents later difficulties.

lineTexts.text: Not going out of the gate and the courtyard brings misfortune.
lineTexts.interpretation: When the time for action comes, excessive limitation becomes harmful. Hesitation when action is called for brings misfortune.

lineTexts.text: He who knows no limitation will have cause to lament. No blame.
lineTexts.interpretation: One who fails to set limits will eventually suffer. The suffering itself, though painful, brings no blame—it teaches the lesson.

lineTexts.text: Contented limitation. Success.
lineTexts.interpretation: Accepting natural limits with contentment brings success. Limits embraced willingly are not experienced as oppression.

lineTexts.text: Sweet limitation brings good fortune. Going brings esteem.
lineTexts.interpretation: Limitation that is experienced as sweet, even pleasant, brings good fortune. Such willing constraint earns respect when one acts.

lineTexts.text: Galling limitation. Perseverance brings misfortune. Remorse disappears.
lineTexts.interpretation: Harsh limitation is unsustainable. Persisting in excessive restriction brings misfortune. When limits are relaxed, regret vanishes.

judgment:
INNER TRUTH. Pigs and fishes.
Good fortune.
It furthers one to cross the great water.
Perseverance furthers.
image:
Wind over lake: the image of INNER TRUTH.
Thus the superior man discusses criminal cases
In order to delay executions.
commentary:
Wind blows over the lake and stirs the surface. The effect of the invisible on the visible is the image of inner truth affecting the outer world. The inner truth that moves the heart can influence even difficult creatures like pigs and fish.

Inner truth means genuine understanding and sincerity that comes from within. With such authenticity, one can undertake difficult journeys and influence others who might otherwise be resistant. The power of inner truth is invisible but effective.

lineTexts.text: Being prepared brings good fortune. If there are secret designs, it is disquieting.
lineTexts.interpretation: Readiness based on inner truth brings good fortune. But hidden agendas, even one's own, create unease. Be transparent.

lineTexts.text: A crane calling in the shade. Its young answers it. I have a good goblet. I will share it with you.
lineTexts.interpretation: Inner truth communicates naturally; like calls to like. Genuine feeling evokes genuine response. Sharing freely what is precious.

lineTexts.text: He finds a comrade. Now he beats the drum, now he stops. Now he sobs, now he sings.
lineTexts.interpretation: Dependence on another for one's emotional state. Sometimes ecstatic, sometimes despairing, according to the other's response. Instability.

lineTexts.text: The moon nearly at the full. The team horse goes astray. No blame.
lineTexts.interpretation: As full development nears, a break with the old partner occurs. This separation, though initially concerning, brings no blame.

lineTexts.text: He possesses truth, which links together. No blame.
lineTexts.interpretation: Inner truth that unifies, drawing others together. Being the center of a genuine connection is without blame.

lineTexts.text: Cockcrow penetrating to heaven. Perseverance brings misfortune.
lineTexts.interpretation: The rooster's crow is sincere but ineffective at reaching heaven. Genuine expression that overreaches its appropriate sphere brings misfortune.

judgment:
PREPONDERANCE OF THE SMALL. Success.
Perseverance furthers.
Small things may be done; great things should not be done.
The flying bird brings the message:
It is not well to strive upward,
It is well to remain below.
Great good fortune.
image:
Thunder on the mountain:
The image of PREPONDERANCE OF THE SMALL.
Thus in his conduct the superior man gives preponderance to reverence.
In bereavement he gives preponderance to grief.
In his expenditures he gives preponderance to thrift.
commentary:
This hexagram depicts a bird in flight: the two yang lines in the center are the body, the yin lines above and below are the wings. But the bird has too much wing for its body—the small predominates.

This is a time when ordinary affairs succeed but great undertakings should not be attempted. Like a bird that should stay low rather than soaring high, one should attend to small matters with care rather than reaching for the extraordinary. In such times, modesty and attention to detail bring success.

lineTexts.text: The bird meets with misfortune through flying.
lineTexts.interpretation: Trying to fly too soon or too high brings misfortune. At the beginning, one is not ready for ambitious action.

lineTexts.text: She passes by her ancestor and meets her ancestress. He does not reach his prince and meets the official. No blame.
lineTexts.interpretation: Not reaching the highest goal but connecting with what is accessible and appropriate. Missing the target but hitting something valuable. No blame.

lineTexts.text: If one is not extremely careful, somebody may come from behind and strike him. Misfortune.
lineTexts.interpretation: Proceeding without adequate caution invites attack from behind. Excessive confidence in a time calling for vigilance brings misfortune.

lineTexts.text: No blame. He meets him without passing by. Going brings danger. One must be on guard. Do not act. Be constantly persevering.
lineTexts.interpretation: Meeting appropriately without overreaching. The situation is dangerous; action is not advised. Maintain watchfulness and constancy.

lineTexts.text: Dense clouds, no rain from our western territory. The prince shoots and hits him who is in the cave.
lineTexts.interpretation: Much preparation but no fulfillment. However, targeted action from an authoritative position succeeds in reaching what is hidden.

lineTexts.text: He passes him by, not meeting him. The flying bird leaves him. Misfortune. This means bad luck and injury.
lineTexts.interpretation: Overreaching and missing the connection. Like a bird flying too far, one goes beyond the mark. Misfortune and harm result.

judgment:
AFTER COMPLETION. Success in small matters.
Perseverance furthers.
At the beginning good fortune,
At the end disorder.
image:
Water over fire: the image of the condition
In AFTER COMPLETION.
Thus the superior man
Takes thought of misfortune
And arms himself against it in advance.
commentary:
This hexagram is the evolution of Tai, Peace. The transition from confusion to order is completed, and everything is in its proper place. Water over fire: fire heats the water and water cools the fire—perfect balance.

But this perfect order carries within it the seeds of decay. When everything is in order, the tendency is toward decline. The beginning is favorable, but the end threatens disorder. The wise person, understanding this, remains vigilant even in times of order.

lineTexts.text: He brakes his wheels. He gets his tail in the water. No blame.
lineTexts.interpretation: At the moment of completion, the cautious person restrains momentum. Like a fox who gets his tail wet crossing a stream—minor mishap from caution. No blame.

lineTexts.text: The woman loses the curtain of her carriage. Do not run after it; on the seventh day you will get it.
lineTexts.interpretation: Something of value is lost but should not be pursued. It will return in due time. Let things take their natural course.

lineTexts.text: The Illustrious Ancestor disciplines the Devil's Country. After three years he conquers it. Inferior people must not be employed.
lineTexts.interpretation: Completing a long, difficult campaign. Success after sustained effort, but care must be taken about who is entrusted with new responsibilities.

lineTexts.text: The finest clothes turn to rags. Be careful all day long.
lineTexts.interpretation: Even the best conditions deteriorate. Constant vigilance is necessary to maintain what has been achieved. Careful attention all day long.

lineTexts.text: The neighbor in the east who slaughters an ox does not attain as much real happiness as the neighbor in the west with his small offering.
lineTexts.interpretation: Genuine sincerity in a modest offering brings more real blessing than elaborate ceremony without heart. Substance over show.

lineTexts.text: He gets his head in the water. Danger.
lineTexts.interpretation: After completion, one ventures too far and gets in over their head. The fox that got his tail wet now gets his head wet. Danger from overconfidence.

judgment:
BEFORE COMPLETION. Success.
But if the little fox, after nearly completing the crossing,
Gets his tail in the water,
There is nothing that would further.
image:
Fire over water:
The image of the condition before transition.
Thus the superior man is careful
In the differentiation of things,
So that each finds its place.
commentary:
This hexagram represents the final phase before completion—the moment of transition that has not yet occurred. Unlike After Completion, where fire heats water appropriately, here fire rises above water and they do not interact productively.

But transition is imminent. The situation calls for careful discrimination about what belongs where. The little fox almost completes the crossing—only at the very last moment does carelessness undo all the effort. The moral: maintain vigilance through the final steps.

lineTexts.text: He gets his tail in the water. Humiliating.
lineTexts.interpretation: Rushing forward before the time is right leads to embarrassment. Like the fox who wades in too eagerly. Patience needed.

lineTexts.text: He brakes his wheels. Perseverance brings good fortune.
lineTexts.interpretation: Holding back, restraining forward momentum. This patience and perseverance at the right moment brings good fortune.

lineTexts.text: Before completion, attack brings misfortune. It furthers one to cross the great water.
lineTexts.interpretation: The transition is not yet complete; aggressive action now brings misfortune. But the great crossing itself is favored—the goal is right.

lineTexts.text: Perseverance brings good fortune. Remorse disappears. Shock, thus to discipline the Devil's Country. For three years, great realms are awarded.
lineTexts.interpretation: Steady effort brings good fortune and dissolves regret. Vigorous action to complete a great task is eventually rewarded with lasting recognition.

lineTexts.text: Perseverance brings good fortune. No remorse. The light of the superior man is true. Good fortune.
lineTexts.interpretation: The genuine radiance of inner truth shines forth. Perseverance brings good fortune without regret. Authentic character is recognized.

lineTexts.text: There is drinking of wine in genuine confidence. No blame. But if one wets his head, he loses it, in truth.
lineTexts.interpretation: Celebrating in genuine confidence is without blame. But excess—getting one's head wet like the careless fox—leads to losing everything. Know when to stop.
